title: protea angolensis var. trichanthera
intro: Protea angolensis var. trichanthera, also known as the Fever Tree, is a shrub-like plant native to southern Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its therapeutic properties.
